name: SYNB1618
summary: SYNB1618 is an engineered E. coli live biotherapeutic normalized for human phenylalanine pharmacodynamic evidence.
mechanism_summary: Uses engineered bacterial metabolism to consume or transform phenylalanine, with linked evidence bounded to PKU pharmacodynamics and tolerability.

open_questions: Whether the linked evidence generalizes beyond the specific population, model, tissue, or disease context already recorded in the public finding. Linked context: SYNB1618 phenylketonuria first-in-human phase 1/2a study; SYNB1618 showed human tolerability and phenylalanine-metabolite pharmacodynamics.
Whether mechanism-specific effects can be separated from combination, product, delivery, or study-design confounding.
Whether future studies can connect the observed biomarker, functional, or model-organism signal to durable aging-relevant outcomes.
